title: Implement serde attribute integration, override attributes, and compile-time validation
purpose: Complete the ToolArgs derive macro by adding full serde attribute integration (rename, rename_all, flatten), the #[tool_args(...)] escape-hatch attributes for manual schema overrides, and compile-time validation that enforces doc comments on all schema-visible fields. After this brief, the derive macro handles every pattern needed for the existing tool codebase.
task: Extend tool_args/parse.rs to extract serde rename, rename_all, skip_serializing, and flatten attributes. Extend tool_args/schema.rs to apply rename transformations and flatten nested struct properties. Create tool_args/validate.rs for compile-time checks. Implement #[tool_args(schema = {...})], #[tool_args(description = "...")], #[tool_args(skip)], #[tool_args(required)], and #[tool_args(additional_properties)] override attributes. Add compile_error! for fields missing doc comments.

requirements.title: Parse serde rename attributes
requirements.spec: The parser SHALL extract #[serde(rename = "x")] on fields and #[serde(rename_all = "...")] on structs. The rename_all value SHALL be parsed as a case-transformation rule supporting: lowercase, UPPERCASE, camelCase, snake_case, PascalCase, SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE, kebab-case, SCREAMING-KEBAB-CASE.
acceptance: #[serde(rename = "custom_name")] on a field records "custom_name" as the schema property name
#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")] on a struct records camelCase as the container-level rename rule
All 8 rename_all case transformations are supported
An unrecognized rename_all value produces compile_error!

requirements.title: Apply rename transformations in schema generation
requirements.spec: WHEN a field has #[serde(rename = "x")], the schema property name SHALL be "x" instead of the Rust field name. WHEN the struct has #[serde(rename_all = "...")], all field names SHALL be transformed according to the case rule. Field-level rename SHALL take precedence over struct-level rename_all.
acceptance: A struct with #[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")] and field my_field produces property "myField"
A field with #[serde(rename = "custom")] produces property "custom" regardless of rename_all
The required array uses the renamed names, not the Rust field names
A struct with rename_all = "snake_case" and field MyField produces property "my_field"

requirements.title: Handle skip_serializing and skip_deserializing distinction
requirements.spec: #[serde(skip_serializing)] on a field SHALL keep the field in the schema because it still deserializes. #[serde(skip)] and #[serde(skip_deserializing)] SHALL omit the field from the schema entirely. This distinction SHALL be correct: skip_serializing fields accept model input, skip_deserializing fields do not.
acceptance: A field with #[serde(skip_serializing)] appears in the schema properties and may appear in required
A field with #[serde(skip)] does not appear in properties
A field with #[serde(skip_deserializing)] does not appear in properties
A field with both #[serde(skip_serializing)] and #[serde(skip_deserializing)] does not appear in properties

requirements.title: Implement serde flatten support
requirements.spec: WHEN a field has #[serde(flatten)], the macro SHALL merge the flattened struct's properties into the parent schema instead of nesting them. The flattened struct's required fields SHALL be added to the parent's required array. IF #[serde(flatten)] is applied to a non-struct type, the macro SHALL emit compile_error!.
acceptance: A struct with a #[serde(flatten)] field of type Inner merges Inner's properties into the parent properties
Inner's required fields appear in the parent's required array
The flattened field itself does not appear as a named property
#[serde(flatten)] on a field of type String produces compile_error! mentioning that flatten requires a struct type
The error span points to the flatten attribute

requirements.title: Implement #[tool_args(...)] override attributes
requirements.spec: The parser SHALL recognize #[tool_args(schema = {...})], #[tool_args(description = "...")], #[tool_args(skip)], #[tool_args(required)], and #[tool_args(additional_properties)] attributes on fields. schema replaces the generated schema entirely. description overrides the doc-comment description. skip excludes from schema without affecting deserialization. required forces inclusion in required even with #[serde(default)]. additional_properties sets additionalProperties: true on the field's object schema.
acceptance: #[tool_args(schema = {"type": "string", "enum": ["strict", "auto"]})] produces the literal JSON as the field schema
#[tool_args(description = "Custom desc")] overrides the doc comment
#[tool_args(skip)] excludes the field from properties without adding #[serde(skip)]
#[tool_args(required)] on a #[serde(default)] field puts the field back in required
#[tool_args(additional_properties)] on a struct field adds "additionalProperties": true to that field's schema

requirements.title: Compile-time doc comment enforcement
requirements.spec: IF a field has no doc comment AND no #[tool_args(skip)] AND no #[serde(skip)] AND no #[serde(skip_deserializing)], THEN the macro SHALL emit compile_error!("ToolArgs: field `{name}` has no doc comment. Add a /// comment to provide the schema description."). The error SHALL point to the field via syn::Error::new_spanned.
acceptance: A field without a doc comment and without skip produces a compile error naming the field
A field with #[tool_args(skip)] but no doc comment does not produce an error
A field with #[serde(skip)] but no doc comment does not produce an error
A field with #[tool_args(description = "...")] but no doc comment does not produce an error
The error message includes the field name
The error span highlights the field, not the struct or derive

requirements.title: Verify derive does not interfere with Deserialize
requirements.spec: The ToolArgs derive SHALL NOT modify the struct definition, add attributes, or conflict with #[derive(Deserialize)]. Both derives SHALL be applicable to the same struct independently. The ToolArgs derive reads serde attributes but does not write them.
acceptance: A struct with #[derive(Deserialize, ToolArgs)] compiles without errors
Deserializing a JSON object into the struct works identically with and without ToolArgs
A struct with #[derive(ToolArgs)] only (no Deserialize) also compiles
The generated json_schema() method does not conflict with any serde-generated methods

requirements.title: Integration tests for serde attributes, overrides, and validation
requirements.spec: Integration tests SHALL verify all serde attribute handling, override attributes, and compile-time validation. Compile-fail tests SHALL verify that missing doc comments and flatten on non-structs produce the expected errors.
acceptance: Test: #[serde(rename = "x")] produces property "x"
Test: #[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")] transforms all property names
Test: #[serde(flatten)] merges properties from inner struct
Test: #[tool_args(schema = {...})] replaces the field schema
Test: #[tool_args(description = "...")] overrides doc comment
Test: #[tool_args(required)] overrides #[serde(default)]
Test: field without doc comment produces compile_error!
Test: #[derive(Deserialize, ToolArgs)] on same struct compiles and both work
All tests pass with cargo test -p norn-macros

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement the tool_follow_ups! macro — that is NTM-004
SHALL NOT modify any existing tool implementations — migration is NTM-005
SHALL NOT implement custom deserialize_with schema inference — fields with custom deserializers use their declared Rust type for schema, or #[tool_args(schema = {...})] for override
SHALL NOT support serde(with = "...") for schema generation — the macro uses the field's declared type
verification: cargo check -p norn-macros succeeds with zero errors
cargo clippy -p norn-macros -- -D warnings produces zero errors
cargo test -p norn-macros passes all tests
A struct combining rename_all, flatten, tool_args overrides, and doc comments produces the expected schema
A struct with a doc-less field (and no skip) fails to compile with a clear error pointing to the field
#[derive(Deserialize, ToolArgs)] on the same struct compiles and both derives produce correct behavior
